name: SIGMA - Network Connection Initiated By AddinUtil.EXE
author: Michael McKinley (@McKinleyMike), Tony Latteri (@TheLatteri)
description:
Detects a network connection initiated by the Add-In deployment cache updating utility "AddInutil.exe".
This could indicate a potential command and control communication as this tool doesn't usually initiate network activity.
false_positives: Unknown
query: any where EventID:3 and (Initiated:"true" and Image:"*\\addinutil.exe")

name: SIGMA - Uncommon Connection to Active Directory Web Services
author: @kostastsale
description: Detects uncommon network connections to the Active Directory Web Services (ADWS) from processes not typically associated with ADWS management.
false_positives: ADWS is used by a number of legitimate applications that need to interact with Active Directory. These applications should be added to the allow-listing to avoid false positives.
query: any where EventID:3 and ((Initiated:true and DestinationPort:9389) and (not (Image:"C:\\Windows\\system32\\dsac.exe" or Image:"C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft Monitoring Agent\\" or (Image like~ ("C:\\Program Files\\PowerShell\\7\\pwsh.exe*", "C:\\Program Files\\PowerShell\\7-preview\\pwsh.ex*", "C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\*", "C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\WindowsPowerShell\\*")))))

name: SIGMA - Uncommon Network Connection Initiated By Certutil.EXE
author: frack113, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects a network connection initiated by the certutil.exe utility.
Attackers can abuse the utility in order to download malware or additional payloads.
false_positives: Unknown
query: any where EventID:3 and (Image:"*\\certutil.exe" and Initiated:"true" and (DestinationPort like~ (80, 135, 443, 445)))

name: SIGMA - Outbound Network Connection Initiated By Cmstp.EXE
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects a network connection initiated by Cmstp.EXE
Its uncommon for "cmstp.exe" to initiate an outbound network connection. Investigate the source of such requests to determine if they are malicious.
false_positives: Unknown
query: any where EventID:3 and ((Image:"*\\cmstp.exe" and Initiated:"true") and (not (cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "127.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "10.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "172.16.0.0/12") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "192.168.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "169.254.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "::1/128") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "fe80::/10") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "fc00::/7"))))

name: SIGMA - Outbound Network Connection Initiated By Microsoft Dialer
author: CertainlyP
description:
Detects outbound network connection initiated by Microsoft Dialer.
The Microsoft Dialer, also known as Phone Dialer, is a built-in utility application included in various versions of the Microsoft Windows operating system. Its primary function is to provide users with a graphical interface for managing phone calls via a modem or a phone line connected to the computer.
This is an outdated process in the current conext of it's usage and is a common target for info stealers for process injection, and is used to make C2 connections, common example is "Rhadamanthys"
false_positives: In Modern Windows systems, unable to see legitimate usage of this process, However, if an organization has legitimate purpose for this there can be false positives.
query: any where EventID:3 and ((Image:"*:\\Windows\\System32\\dialer.exe" and Initiated:"true") and (not (cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "127.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "10.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "172.16.0.0/12") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "192.168.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "169.254.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "::1/128") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "fe80::/10") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "fc00::/7"))))

name: SIGMA - Network Connection Initiated To BTunnels Domains
author: Kamran Saifullah
description:
Detects network connections to BTunnels domains initiated by a process on the system.
Attackers can abuse that feature to establish a reverse shell or persistence on a machine.
false_positives: Legitimate use of BTunnels will also trigger this.
query: any where EventID:3 and (Initiated:"true" and DestinationHostname:"*.btunnel.co.in")

name: SIGMA - Network Connection Initiated To Cloudflared Tunnels Domains
author: Kamran Saifullah, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects network connections to Cloudflared tunnels domains initiated by a process on the system.
Attackers can abuse that feature to establish a reverse shell or persistence on a machine.
false_positives: Legitimate use of cloudflare tunnels will also trigger this.
query: any where EventID:3 and (Initiated:"true" and (DestinationHostname like~ ("*.v2.argotunnel.com", "*protocol-v2.argotunnel.com", "*trycloudflare.com", "*update.argotunnel.com")))

In this context attackers leverage known websites such as "facebook", "youtube", etc. In order to pass through undetected.

name: SIGMA - Network Connection Initiated To DevTunnels Domain
author: Kamran Saifullah
description: Detects network connections to Devtunnels domains initiated by a process on a system. Attackers can abuse that feature to establish a reverse shell or persistence on a machine.
false_positives: Legitimate use of Devtunnels will also trigger this.
query: any where EventID:3 and (Initiated:"true" and DestinationHostname:"*.devtunnels.ms")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Dropbox API Usage
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects an executable that isn't dropbox but communicates with the Dropbox API
false_positives: Legitimate use of the API with a tool that the author wasn't aware of
query: any where EventID:3 and ((Initiated:"true" and (DestinationHostname like~ ("*api.dropboxapi.com", "*content.dropboxapi.com"))) and (not Image:"*\\Dropbox*"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Network Connection to IP Lookup Service APIs

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Non-Browser Network Communication With Google API
author: Gavin Knapp
description: Detects a non-browser process interacting with the Google API which could indicate the use of a covert C2 such as Google Sheet C2 (GC2-sheet)
false_positives: Legitimate applications communicating with the "googleapis.com" endpoints that are not already in the exclusion list. This is environmental dependent and requires further testing and tuning.
query: any where EventID:3 and ((DestinationHostname like~ ("*drive.googleapis.com*", "*oauth2.googleapis.com*", "*sheets.googleapis.com*", "*www.googleapis.com*")) and (not ((?Image == null) or Image=="")) and (not (Image:"*\\brave.exe" or (Image like~ ("*:\\Program Files\\Google\\Chrome\\Application\\chrome.exe", "*:\\Program Files (x86)\\Google\\Chrome\\Application\\chrome.exe")) or (Image:"*:\\Program Files\\Google\\Drive File Stream\\*" and Image:"*\\GoogleDriveFS.exe") or (Image like~ ("*:\\Program Files\\Mozilla Firefox\\firefox.exe", "*:\\Program Files (x86)\\Mozilla Firefox\\firefox.exe")) or (Image like~ ("*:\\Program Files (x86)\\Internet Explorer\\iexplore.exe", "*:\\Program Files\\Internet Explorer\\iexplore.exe")) or Image:"*\\maxthon.exe" or (Image:"*:\\Program Files (x86)\\Microsoft\\EdgeWebView\\Application\\*" or (Image like~ ("*:\\Program Files (x86)\\Microsoft\\Edge\\Application\\msedge.exe", "*:\\Program Files\\Microsoft\\Edge\\Application\\msedge.exe", "*\\WindowsApps\\MicrosoftEdge.exe"))) or ((Image like~ ("*:\\Program Files (x86)\\Microsoft\\EdgeCore\\*", "*:\\Program Files\\Microsoft\\EdgeCore\\*")) and (Image like~ ("*\\msedge.exe", "*\\msedgewebview2.exe"))) or Image:"*\\opera.exe" or Image:"*\\safari.exe" or Image:"*\\seamonkey.exe" or Image:"*\\vivaldi.exe" or Image:"*\\whale.exe" or Image:"*\\GoogleUpdate.exe" or Image:"*\\outlook.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Communication To LocaltoNet Tunneling Service Initiated
author: Andreas Braathen (mnemonic.io)
description:
Detects an executable initiating a network connection to "LocaltoNet" tunneling sub-domains.
LocaltoNet is a reverse proxy that enables localhost services to be exposed to the Internet.
Attackers have been seen to use this service for command-and-control activities to bypass MFA and perimeter controls.
false_positives: Legitimate use of the LocaltoNet service.
query: any where EventID:3 and ((DestinationHostname like~ ("*.localto.net", "*.localtonet.com")) and Initiated:"true")

name: SIGMA - Network Connection Initiated To Mega.nz
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects a network connection initiated by a binary to "api.mega.co.nz".
Attackers were seen abusing file sharing websites similar to "mega.nz" in order to upload/download additional payloads.
false_positives: Legitimate MEGA installers and utilities are expected to communicate with this domain. Exclude hosts that are known to be allowed to use this tool.
query: any where EventID:3 and (Initiated:"true" and (DestinationHostname like~ ("*mega.co.nz", "*mega.nz")))

name: SIGMA - Process Initiated Network Connection To Ngrok Domain
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects an executable initiating a network connection to "ngrok" domains.
Attackers were seen using this "ngrok" in order to store their second stage payloads and malware.
While communication with such domains can be legitimate, often times is a sign of either data exfiltration by malicious actors or additional download.
false_positives: Legitimate use of the ngrok service.
query: any where EventID:3 and (Initiated:"true" and (DestinationHostname like~ ("*.ngrok-free.app", "*.ngrok-free.dev", "*.ngrok.app", "*.ngrok.dev", "*.ngrok.io")))

name: SIGMA - Communication To Ngrok Tunneling Service Initiated
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects an executable initiating a network connection to "ngrok" tunneling domains.
Attackers were seen using this "ngrok" in order to store their second stage payloads and malware.
While communication with such domains can be legitimate, often times is a sign of either data exfiltration by malicious actors or additional download.
false_positives: Legitimate use of the ngrok service.
query: any where EventID:3 and (DestinationHostname like~ ("*tunnel.us.ngrok.com*", "*tunnel.eu.ngrok.com*", "*tunnel.ap.ngrok.com*", "*tunnel.au.ngrok.com*", "*tunnel.sa.ngrok.com*", "*tunnel.jp.ngrok.com*", "*tunnel.in.ngrok.com*"))

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Network Connection To Notion API
author: Gavin Knapp
description: Detects a non-browser process communicating with the Notion API. This could indicate potential use of a covert C2 channel such as "OffensiveNotion C2"
false_positives: Legitimate applications communicating with the "api.notion.com" endpoint that are not already in the exclusion list. The desktop and browser applications do not appear to be using the API by default unless integrations are configured.
query: any where EventID:3 and (DestinationHostname:"*api.notion.com*" and (not (Image:"*\\AppData\\Local\\Programs\\Notion\\Notion.exe" or Image:"*\\brave.exe" or (Image like~ ("C:\\Program Files\\Google\\Chrome\\Application\\chrome.exe", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Google\\Chrome\\Application\\chrome.exe")) or (Image like~ ("C:\\Program Files\\Mozilla Firefox\\firefox.exe", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Mozilla Firefox\\firefox.exe")) or (Image like~ ("C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Internet Explorer\\iexplore.exe", "C:\\Program Files\\Internet Explorer\\iexplore.exe")) or Image:"*\\maxthon.exe" or (Image:"C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Microsoft\\EdgeWebView\\Application\\*" or Image:"*\\WindowsApps\\MicrosoftEdge.exe" or (Image like~ ("C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Microsoft\\Edge\\Application\\msedge.exe", "C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft\\Edge\\Application\\msedge.exe"))) or ((Image like~ ("C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Microsoft\\EdgeCore\\*", "C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft\\EdgeCore\\*")) and (Image like~ ("*\\msedge.exe", "*\\msedgewebview2.exe"))) or Image:"*\\opera.exe" or Image:"*\\safari.exe" or Image:"*\\seamonkey.exe" or Image:"*\\vivaldi.exe" or Image:"*\\whale.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Network Communication Initiated To Portmap.IO Domain
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects an executable accessing the portmap.io domain, which could be a sign of forbidden C2 traffic or data exfiltration by malicious actors
false_positives: Legitimate use of portmap.io domains
query: any where EventID:3 and (Initiated:"true" and DestinationHostname:"*.portmap.io")

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Non-Browser Network Communication With Telegram API
author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects an a non-browser process interacting with the Telegram API which could indicate use of a covert C2
false_positives: Legitimate applications communicating with the Telegram API e.g. web browsers not in the exclusion list, app with an RSS  etc.
query: any where EventID:3 and (DestinationHostname:"*api.telegram.org*" and (not (Image:"*\\brave.exe" or (Image like~ ("C:\\Program Files\\Google\\Chrome\\Application\\chrome.exe", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Google\\Chrome\\Application\\chrome.exe")) or (Image like~ ("C:\\Program Files\\Mozilla Firefox\\firefox.exe", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Mozilla Firefox\\firefox.exe")) or (Image like~ ("C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Internet Explorer\\iexplore.exe", "C:\\Program Files\\Internet Explorer\\iexplore.exe")) or Image:"*\\maxthon.exe" or (Image:"C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Microsoft\\EdgeWebView\\Application\\*" or Image:"*\\WindowsApps\\MicrosoftEdge.exe" or (Image like~ ("C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Microsoft\\Edge\\Application\\msedge.exe", "C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft\\Edge\\Application\\msedge.exe"))) or ((Image like~ ("C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Microsoft\\EdgeCore\\*", "C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft\\EdgeCore\\*")) and (Image like~ ("*\\msedge.exe", "*\\msedgewebview2.exe"))) or Image:"*\\opera.exe" or Image:"*\\safari.exe" or Image:"*\\seamonkey.exe" or Image:"*\\vivaldi.exe" or Image:"*\\whale.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Network Connection Initiated To Visual Studio Code Tunnels Domain
author: Kamran Saifullah
description: Detects network connections to Visual Studio Code tunnel domains initiated by a process on a system. Attackers can abuse that feature to establish a reverse shell or persistence on a machine.
false_positives: Legitimate use of Visual Studio Code tunnel will also trigger this.
query: any where EventID:3 and (Initiated:"true" and DestinationHostname:"*.tunnels.api.visualstudio.com")

name: SIGMA - Network Connection Initiated By Eqnedt32.EXE
author: Max Altgelt (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects network connections from the Equation Editor process "eqnedt32.exe".
false_positives: Unlikely
query: any where EventID:3 and Image:"*\\eqnedt32.exe"

name: SIGMA - Network Connection Initiated By IMEWDBLD.EXE
author: frack113
description: Detects a network connection initiated by IMEWDBLD.EXE. This might indicate potential abuse of the utility as a LOLBIN in order to download arbitrary files or additional payloads.
false_positives: Unknown
query: any where EventID:3 and (Initiated:"true" and Image:"*\\IMEWDBLD.exe")

name: SIGMA - Network Connection Initiated Via Notepad.EXE
author: EagleEye Team
description:
Detects a network connection that is initiated by the "notepad.exe" process.
This might be a sign of process injection from a beacon process or something similar.
Notepad rarely initiates a network communication except when printing documents for example.
false_positives: Printing documents via notepad might cause communication with the printer via port 9100 or similar.
query: any where EventID:3 and (Image:"*\\notepad.exe" and (not DestinationPort:9100))

name: SIGMA - Office Application Initiated Network Connection Over Uncommon Ports
author: X__Junior (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects an office suit application (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook) communicating to target systems over uncommon ports.
false_positives: Other ports can be used, apply additional filters accordingly
query: any where EventID:3 and ((Initiated:"true" and (Image like~ ("*\\excel.exe", "*\\outlook.exe", "*\\powerpnt.exe", "*\\winword.exe", "*\\wordview.exe"))) and (not ((DestinationPort like~ (53, 80, 139, 389, 443, 445, 3268)) or (Image:"*:\\Program Files\\Microsoft Office\\*" and Image:"*\\OUTLOOK.EXE" and (DestinationPort like~ (143, 465, 587, 993, 995))))))

name: SIGMA - Python Initiated Connection
author: frack113
description: Detects a Python process initiating a network connection. While this often relates to package installation, it can also indicate a potential malicious script communicating with a C&C server.
false_positives: Legitimate python scripts using the socket library or similar will trigger this. Apply additional filters and perform an initial baseline before deploying.
query: any where EventID:3 and ((Initiated:"true" and (Image:"*\\python*" and Image:"*.exe*")) and (not ((DestinationIp=="127.0.0.1" and SourceIp=="127.0.0.1") or (CommandLine:"*pip.exe*" and CommandLine:"*install*"))) and (not ((ParentImage:"C:\\ProgramData\\Anaconda3\\Scripts\\conda.exe" and (CommandLine:"*:\\ProgramData\\Anaconda3\\Scripts\\conda-script.py*" and CommandLine:"*update*")) or (ParentImage:"C:\\ProgramData\\Anaconda3\\python.exe" and CommandLine:"*C:\\ProgramData\\Anaconda3\\Scripts\\jupyter-notebook-script.py*"))))

name: SIGMA - Outbound RDP Connections Over Non-Standard Tools
author: Markus Neis
description:
Detects Non-Standard tools initiating a connection over port 3389 indicating possible lateral movement.
An initial baseline is required before using this utility to exclude third party RDP tooling that you might use.
false_positives: Third party RDP tools
query: any where EventID:3 and ((DestinationPort:3389 and Initiated:"true") and (not (Image like~ ("C:\\Windows\\System32\\mstsc.exe", "C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\mstsc.exe"))) and (not ((Image:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\dns.exe" and SourcePort:53 and Protocol:"udp") or (Image like~ ("*\\Avast Software\\Avast\\AvastSvc.exe", "*\\Avast\\AvastSvc.exe")) or Image:"*\\RDCMan.exe" or Image:"C:\\Program Files\\Google\\Chrome\\Application\\chrome.exe" or (Image like~ ("*\\FSAssessment.exe", "*\\FSDiscovery.exe", "*\\MobaRTE.exe", "*\\mRemote.exe", "*\\mRemoteNG.exe", "*\\Passwordstate.exe", "*\\RemoteDesktopManager.exe", "*\\RemoteDesktopManager64.exe", "*\\RemoteDesktopManagerFree.exe", "*\\RSSensor.exe", "*\\RTS2App.exe", "*\\RTSApp.exe", "*\\spiceworks-finder.exe", "*\\Terminals.exe", "*\\ws_TunnelService.exe")) or (Image like~ ("*\\thor.exe", "*\\thor64.exe")) or Image:"C:\\Program Files\\SplunkUniversalForwarder\\bin\\*" or Image:"*\\Ranger\\SentinelRanger.exe" or Image:"C:\\Program Files\\Mozilla Firefox\\firefox.exe" or (Image like~ ("C:\\Program Files\\TSplus\\Java\\bin\\HTML5service.exe", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\TSplus\\Java\\bin\\HTML5service.exe")) or (?Image == null) or Image=="" or Image:"<unknown process>")))

name: SIGMA - RDP Over Reverse SSH Tunnel
author: Samir Bousseaden
description: Detects svchost hosting RDP termsvcs communicating with the loopback address and on TCP port 3389
false_positives: Unknown
query: any where EventID:3 and ((Image:"*\\svchost.exe" and Initiated:"true" and SourcePort:3389) and (cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "127.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "::1/128")))

name: SIGMA - RDP to HTTP or HTTPS Target Ports
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects svchost hosting RDP termsvcs communicating to target systems on TCP port 80 or 443
false_positives: Unknown
query: any where EventID:3 and (Image:"*\\svchost.exe" and Initiated:"true" and SourcePort:3389 and (DestinationPort like~ (80, 443)))

name: SIGMA - RegAsm.EXE Initiating Network Connection To Public IP
author: frack113
description: Detects "RegAsm.exe" initiating a network connection to public IP adresses
false_positives: Unknown
query: any where EventID:3 and ((Initiated:"true" and Image:"*\\regasm.exe") and (not (cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "127.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "10.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "172.16.0.0/12") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "192.168.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "169.254.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "::1/128") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "fe80::/10") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "fc00::/7"))))

name: SIGMA - Network Connection Initiated By Regsvr32.EXE
author: Dmitriy Lifanov, oscd.community
description: Detects a network connection initiated by "Regsvr32.exe"
false_positives: Unknown
query: any where EventID:3 and (Initiated:"true" and Image:"*\\regsvr32.exe")

name: SIGMA - Remote Access Tool - AnyDesk Incoming Connection
author: @d4ns4n_ (Wuerth-Phoenix)
description: Detects incoming connections to AnyDesk. This could indicate a potential remote attacker trying to connect to a listening instance of AnyDesk and use it as potential command and control channel.
false_positives: Legitimate incoming connections (e.g. sysadmin activity). Most of the time I would expect outgoing connections (initiated locally).
query: any where EventID:3 and ((Image like~ ("*\\AnyDesk.exe", "*\\AnyDeskMSI.exe")) and Initiated:"false")

name: SIGMA - Rundll32 Internet Connection
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a rundll32 that communicates with public IP addresses
false_positives: Communication to other corporate systems that use IP addresses from public address spaces
query: any where EventID:3 and ((Image:"*\\rundll32.exe" and Initiated:"true") and (not ((cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "127.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "10.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "172.16.0.0/12") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "192.168.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "169.254.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "::1/128") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "fe80::/10") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "fc00::/7")) or (cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "20.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "51.103.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "51.104.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "51.105.0.0/16")) or CommandLine:"*\\system32\\PcaSvc.dll,PcaPatchSdbTask" or SourceHostname:"*.internal.cloudapp.net" or (ParentImage:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\svchost.exe" and DestinationPort:443))))

name: SIGMA - Silenttrinity Stager Msbuild Activity
author: Kiran kumar s, oscd.community
description: Detects a possible remote connections to Silenttrinity c2
false_positives: Unknown
query: any where EventID:3 and (Image:"*\\msbuild.exe" and ((DestinationPort like~ (80, 443)) and Initiated:"true"))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Network Connection Binary No CommandLine
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects suspicious network connections made by a well-known Windows binary run with no command line parameters
false_positives: Unknown
query: any where EventID:3 and ((Initiated:"true" and (Image like~ ("*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\dllhost.exe")) and (CommandLine like~ ("*\\regsvr32.exe", "*\\rundll32.exe", "*\\dllhost.exe"))) and (not (CommandLine=="" or (?CommandLine == null))))

name: SIGMA - Network Communication Initiated To File Sharing Domains From Process Located In Suspicious Folder
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects executables located in potentially suspicious directories initiating network connections towards file sharing domains.
false_positives: Some installers located in the temp directory might communicate with the Github domains in order to download additional software. Baseline these cases or move the github domain to a lower level hunting rule.
query: any where EventID:3 and ((Image like~ ("*:\\$Recycle.bin*", "*:\\Perflogs\\*", "*:\\Temp\\*", "*:\\Users\\Default\\*", "*:\\Users\\Public\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Fonts\\*", "*:\\Windows\\IME\\*", "*:\\Windows\\System32\\Tasks\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Tasks\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Temp\\*", "*\\AppData\\Temp\\*", "*\\config\\systemprofile\\*", "*\\Windows\\addins\\*")) and (Initiated:"true" and (DestinationHostname like~ ("*.githubusercontent.com", "*anonfiles.com", "*cdn.discordapp.com", "*ddns.net", "*dl.dropboxusercontent.com", "*ghostbin.co", "*glitch.me", "*gofile.io", "*hastebin.com", "*mediafire.com", "*mega.co.nz", "*mega.nz", "*onrender.com", "*pages.dev", "*paste.ee", "*pastebin.com", "*pastebin.pl", "*pastetext.net", "*pixeldrain.com", "*privatlab.com", "*privatlab.net", "*send.exploit.in", "*sendspace.com", "*storage.googleapis.com", "*storjshare.io", "*supabase.co", "*temp.sh", "*transfer.sh", "*trycloudflare.com", "*ufile.io", "*w3spaces.com", "*workers.dev"))))

name: SIGMA - Network Connection Initiated From Process Located In Potentially Suspicious Or Uncommon Location
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a network connection initiated by programs or processes running from suspicious or uncommon files system locations.
false_positives: Unknown
query: any where EventID:3 and ((Initiated:"true" and (Image like~ ("*:\\$Recycle.bin*", "*:\\Perflogs\\*", "*:\\Temp\\*", "*:\\Users\\Default\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Fonts\\*", "*:\\Windows\\IME\\*", "*:\\Windows\\System32\\Tasks\\*", "*:\\Windows\\Tasks\\*", "*\\config\\systemprofile\\*", "*\\Windows\\addins\\*"))) and (not (DestinationHostname like~ ("*.githubusercontent.com", "*anonfiles.com", "*cdn.discordapp.com", "*ddns.net", "*dl.dropboxusercontent.com", "*ghostbin.co", "*glitch.me", "*gofile.io", "*hastebin.com", "*mediafire.com", "*mega.co.nz", "*mega.nz", "*onrender.com", "*pages.dev", "*paste.ee", "*pastebin.com", "*pastebin.pl", "*pastetext.net", "*portmap.io", "*privatlab.com", "*privatlab.net", "*send.exploit.in", "*sendspace.com", "*storage.googleapis.com", "*storjshare.io", "*supabase.co", "*temp.sh", "*transfer.sh", "*trycloudflare.com", "*ufile.io", "*w3spaces.com", "*workers.dev"))))

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Malware Callback Communication
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects programs that connect to known malware callback ports based on statistical analysis from two different sandbox system databases
false_positives: Unknown
query: any where EventID:3 and ((Initiated:"true" and (DestinationPort like~ (100, 198, 200, 243, 473, 666, 700, 743, 777, 1443, 1515, 1777, 1817, 1904, 1960, 2443, 2448, 3360, 3675, 3939, 4040, 4433, 4438, 4443, 4444, 4455, 5445, 5552, 5649, 6625, 7210, 7777, 8143, 8843, 9631, 9943, 10101, 12102, 12103, 12322, 13145, 13394, 13504, 13505, 13506, 13507, 14102, 14103, 14154, 49180, 65520, 65535))) and (not (cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "127.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "10.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "172.16.0.0/12") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "192.168.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "169.254.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "::1/128") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "fe80::/10") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "fc00::/7"))) and (not (Image like~ ("C:\\Program Files\\*", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - Communication To Uncommon Destination Ports
author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects programs that connect to uncommon destination ports
false_positives: Unknown
query: any where EventID:3 and ((Initiated:"true" and (DestinationPort like~ (8080, 8888))) and (not (cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "127.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "10.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "172.16.0.0/12") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "192.168.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "169.254.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "::1/128") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "fe80::/10") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "fc00::/7"))) and (not (Image like~ ("C:\\Program Files\\*", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\*"))))

name: SIGMA - Uncommon Outbound Kerberos Connection
author: Ilyas Ochkov, oscd.community
description: Detects uncommon outbound network activity via Kerberos default port indicating possible lateral movement or first stage PrivEsc via delegation.
false_positives: Web Browsers and third party application might generate similar activity. An initial baseline is required.
query: any where EventID:3 and ((DestinationPort:88 and Initiated:"true") and (not Image:"C:\\Windows\\System32\\lsass.exe") and (not ((Image like~ ("C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Google\\Chrome\\Application\\chrome.exe", "C:\\Program Files\\Google\\Chrome\\Application\\chrome.exe")) or (Image like~ ("C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Mozilla Firefox\\firefox.exe", "C:\\Program Files\\Mozilla Firefox\\firefox.exe")) or Image:"*\\tomcat\\bin\\tomcat8.exe")))

name: SIGMA - Microsoft Sync Center Suspicious Network Connections
author: elhoim
description: Detects suspicious connections from Microsoft Sync Center to non-private IPs.
false_positives: Unknown
query: any where EventID:3 and (Image:"*\\mobsync.exe" and (not (cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "127.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "10.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "172.16.0.0/12") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "192.168.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "169.254.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "::1/128") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "fe80::/10") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "fc00::/7"))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Outbound SMTP Connections
author: frack113
description:
Adversaries may steal data by exfiltrating it over an un-encrypted network protocol other than that of the existing command and control channel.
The data may also be sent to an alternate network location from the main command and control server.
false_positives: Other SMTP tools
query: any where EventID:3 and (((DestinationPort like~ (25, 587, 465, 2525)) and Initiated:"true") and (not ((Image like~ ("*\\thunderbird.exe", "*\\outlook.exe")) or Image:"C:\\Program Files\\Microsoft\\Exchange Server\\*" or (Image:"C:\\Program Files\\WindowsApps\\microsoft.windowscommunicationsapps_*" and Image:"*\\HxTsr.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Potential Remote PowerShell Session Initiated
author: Roberto Rodriguez @Cyb3rWard0g
description:
Detects a process that initiated a network connection over ports 5985 or 5986 from a non-network service account.
This could potentially indicates a remote PowerShell connection.
false_positives: Legitimate usage of remote PowerShell, e.g. remote administration and monitoring.
Network Service user name of a not-covered localization
query: any where EventID:3 and (((DestinationPort like~ (5985, 5986)) and Initiated:"true" and SourceIsIpv6:"false") and (not (((User like~ ("*NETWORK SERVICE*", "*NETZWERKDIENST*", "*SERVICIO DE RED*", "*SERVIZIO DI RETE*")) or (User:"*SERVICE R*" and User:"*SEAU*")) or ((SourceIp like~ ("::1", "127.0.0.1")) and (DestinationIp like~ ("::1", "127.0.0.1"))))) and (not (Image like~ ("C:\\Program Files\\Avast Software\\Avast\\AvastSvc.exe", "C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Avast Software\\Avast\\AvastSvc.exe"))))

name: SIGMA - Outbound Network Connection To Public IP Via Winlogon
author: Christopher Peacock @securepeacock, SCYTHE @scythe_io
description: Detects a "winlogon.exe" process that initiate network communications with public IP addresses
false_positives: Communication to other corporate systems that use IP addresses from public address spaces
query: any where EventID:3 and ((Image:"*\\winlogon.exe" and Initiated:"true") and (not (cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "127.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "10.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "172.16.0.0/12") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "192.168.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "169.254.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "::1/128") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "fe80::/10") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "fc00::/7"))))

name: SIGMA - Suspicious Wordpad Outbound Connections
author: X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
description:
Detects a network connection initiated by "wordpad.exe" over uncommon destination ports.
This might indicate potential process injection activity from a beacon or similar mechanisms.
false_positives: Other ports can be used, apply additional filters accordingly
query: any where EventID:3 and ((Initiated:"true" and Image:"*\\wordpad.exe") and (not (DestinationPort like~ (80, 139, 443, 445, 465, 587, 993, 995))))

name: SIGMA - Local Network Connection Initiated By Script Interpreter
author: frack113
description: Detects a script interpreter (Wscript/Cscript) initiating a local network connection to download or execute a script hosted on a shared folder.
false_positives: Legitimate scripts
query: any where EventID:3 and (Initiated:"true" and (Image like~ ("*\\wscript.exe", "*\\cscript.exe")) and (cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "127.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "10.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "172.16.0.0/12") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "192.168.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "169.254.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "::1/128") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "fe80::/10") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "fc00::/7")))

name: SIGMA - Outbound Network Connection Initiated By Script Interpreter
author: frack113, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
description: Detects a script interpreter wscript/cscript opening a network connection to a non-local network. Adversaries may use script to download malicious payloads.
false_positives: Legitimate scripts
query: any where EventID:3 and ((Initiated:"true" and (Image like~ ("*\\wscript.exe", "*\\cscript.exe"))) and (not ((cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "127.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "10.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "172.16.0.0/12") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "192.168.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "169.254.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "::1/128") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "fe80::/10") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "fc00::/7")) or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "20.0.0.0/11"))))

name: SIGMA - Potentially Suspicious Wuauclt Network Connection
author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research)
description:
Detects the use of the Windows Update Client binary (wuauclt.exe) to proxy execute code and making network connections.
One could easily make the DLL spawn a new process and inject to it to proxy the network connection and bypass this rule.
false_positives: Unknown
query: any where EventID:3 and ((Image:"*wuauclt*" and CommandLine:"* /RunHandlerComServer*") and (not ((cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "127.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "10.0.0.0/8") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "169.254.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "172.16.0.0/12") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "192.168.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "::1/128") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "fe80::/10") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "fc00::/7")) or (cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "20.184.0.0/13") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "20.192.0.0/10") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "23.79.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "51.10.0.0/15") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "51.103.0.0/16") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "51.104.0.0/15") or cidrMatch(DestinationIp, "52.224.0.0/11")) or (CommandLine like~ ("*:\\Windows\\UUS\\Packages\\Preview\\amd64\\updatedeploy.dll /ClassId*", "*:\\Windows\\UUS\\amd64\\UpdateDeploy.dll /ClassId*")) or (CommandLine:"*:\\Windows\\WinSxS\\*" and CommandLine:"*\\UpdateDeploy.dll /ClassId *") or (?CommandLine == null) or CommandLine=="")))
